id: P18. 18 课题1 水资源及其利用 第2课时_25
caption: This video is the second lesson of the topic "Water Resources and Its Utilization".The content focuses on the separation principle of water impurity removal, such as water purification methods such as filtration and distillation.After-class review will be arranged at the end of the video.
short_query: water purification methods like filtration and distillation lesson

id: P18. 18 课题1 水资源及其利用 第2课时_21
caption: This video explains the principles and methods of distillation for water resource purification.Through analogy of the steamer phenomenon, the physical change process of water vaporized to boiling point (100°C) and condensed into pure water when heated to cool.The commonly used distillation equipment in the laboratory and its applications are introduced.
short_query: how distillation works to purify water in a lab

caption: This video is about the introduction of teaching on plane mirror imaging.Planar mirror imaging phenomenon is introduced through examples of water reflections and cat-looking mirrors in the National Grand Theater.Focus on proposing and guiding students to learn the characteristics and formation principles of the "image" formed by plane mirrors, laying the foundation for a deep understanding of the imaging laws of plane mirrors.
short_query: how do images form in a flat plane mirror

caption: This video explores the imaging rules of plane mirrors, and guides students to raise inquiry scientific questions about image characteristics (size, position) by observing the position and size of the image when an object is close to the mirror (including common misunderstandings from students).
short_query: plane mirror image characteristics size and position science lesson

caption: This video introduces the plane mirror imaging experimental equipment, including thin glass plates, two identical luminescent bodies, coordinate paper, scale ruler, and right-angle triangle plates, which are used to explore the imaging rules of plane mirrors.
short_query: equipment needed for plane mirror imaging experiment

caption: The video explains the characteristics of plane mirror imaging, explains its virtual image formation principle based on light reflection, and discusses the wide application of plane mirrors in production and life.
short_query: how do plane mirrors form virtual images using reflection

id: P19. 10  课题1 化学与人体健康（第2课时）_1
caption: This video is the second lesson of the topic "Chemistry and Human Health".By analyzing students' improved day-to-day meals, explaining the nutrients in common foods.The focus is on explaining that staple foods are rich in sugar, vegetables and fruits are rich in vitamins, lean meat and dairy products are rich in protein, aiming to help students correctly identify the core nutritional components in their daily diet.
short_query: learning the nutrients in common foods for human health

id: P19. 10  课题1 化学与人体健康（第2课时）_7
caption: Introduce essential constants and trace elements of the human body.Detailed description of the effects of fluorine and iodine on human health: fluorine deficiency causes caries and skeletal fluorosis; iodine deficiency causes goiter and intellectual development disorder.It is pointed out that calcium, sodium, iron and other elements exist in food in the form of inorganic salts.
short_query: trace elements fluorine iodine and inorganic salts in human body

id: P19. 10  课题1 化学与人体健康（第2课时）_5
caption: This video explains that human elements remain relatively stable through metabolism, and imbalance intake will affect health.Oxygen, carbon, hydrocarbon, nitrogen mainly exists in the form of proteins, sugars and other organic matter and water, while other elements exist inorganic salts.The video emphasizes six major nutrients: sugars, proteins, oils, vitamins, water, and inorganic salts.
short_query: essential elements and six major nutrients for human health

caption: This video discusses kinetic energy, gravity potential energy and elastic potential energy.It points out that the elastic potential energy is affected by the degree of deformation and the material.Through examples, the energy forms of objects have, such as kinetic energy for automobile sports, the chandelier suspension has gravity potential energy, and the slingshot deformation has elastic potential energy.
short_query: examples of kinetic gravitational and elastic potential energy

caption: This video explains the relationship between the potential energy of the gravity and height by falling from the watermelon skin; combines the brakes of the bicycle to explain the relationship between the kinetic energy of the speed and the brake distance.Emphasize the importance of energy magnitude to daily safety.
short_query: how height and speed affect potential and kinetic energy

caption: This video explains the concept of energy, including kinetic energy and potential energy.Kinetic energy is the energy of an object's movement, which depends on mass and speed.Potential energy is divided into gravitational potential energy (with mass, highly correlated) and elastic potential energy (with degree of deformation, material).
short_query: explaining kinetic and potential energy including gravitational and elastic

caption: This video explores the factors that affect the resistance of the conductor.Through students' conjecture, the relationship between the conductor resistance and cross-sectional area and length is derived.In combination with known resistance is related to materials, the teaching focus is on designing experiments, exploring and verifying the dependence between conductor resistance and length and cross-sectional area.
short_query: how length and cross sectional area affect conductor resistance

caption: This video explains the hindering effect of conductors on current (resistance) through experimental comparison.It points out that the resistance of different materials (such as copper wire, nickel-chromium alloy wire) is different, and the water flow simulation method is introduced to explain the concept of resistance.
short_query: understanding electrical resistance in different materials water flow analogy

caption: This video explains that the ammeter resistance is extremely small and cannot be directly connected to the power supply; the voltmeter resistance is extremely large, and it is equivalent to opening the circuit into the circuit.Introduce a component with a fixed resistance value - a resistor (fixed resistor) and display its circuit symbols.
short_query: understanding ammeter voltmeter resistance and fixed resistor circuit symbols

caption: This video clip first introduces the concept of integrated circuits (chips) and their applications in electronic devices.Then conductivity and resistance are introduced, and whether there are zero resistance conductors are discussed.The superconducting phenomenon is focused on explaining: Dutch physicist Carmeleonis accidentally discovered in 1911 that the resistance of mercury would become zero at extremely low temperatures, that is, the superconducting state.
short_query: introduction to integrated circuits and the discovery of superconductivity

caption: This video compares the resistance of copper wire and nickel-chromium alloy wire by controlling length and thickness variables.The results show that when the nickel-chromium alloy wire is connected to the circuit, the light bulb becomes dimmed and the current decreases (from 0.26A to 0.2A), proving that its resistance is greater than that of the copper wire.
short_query: comparing resistance of copper and nickel chromium wire

id: P19. 17 电阻的测量（第二课时）_18
caption: This video demonstrates resistance measurement experiments.Two ammeters are used to measure the current of unknown resistor Rx and known resistor R0 respectively, emphasizing the adjustment of the range before acquisition.Five sets of current readings were recorded to provide data support for subsequent calculation of resistance Rx, highlighting experimental data acquisition and processing.
short_query: how to measure unknown resistance using two ammeters

id: P19. 17 电阻的测量（第二课时）_13
caption: Video correction voltage errors caused by circuit changes in resistance measurement.Next, a feasible solution is introduced: through switching operation, the voltage across the known resistor R0 and the resistor Rx to be measured is measured respectively to find Rx.
short_query: how to measure resistance by switching between two resistors

id: P19. 17 电阻的测量（第二课时）_17
caption: The video explains how to measure resistance using a dual ammeter when there is no voltmeter.The principle and practice of designing circuits and collecting data by measuring parallel branch currents is focused on the principle and practice of conducting resistance measurement.
short_query: how to measure resistance with dual ammeters and no voltmeter

id: P19. 17 电阻的测量（第二课时）_10
caption: This video discusses circuit designs that measure unknown resistance with only one voltmeter.Analyze the three proposals proposed by students and evaluate their feasibility and principles, focusing on changing the measurement object through switches.
short_query: using switches and one voltmeter to find unknown resistance

id: P19. 17 电阻的测量（第二课时）_11
caption: This video explains resistance Rx measurement.Based on the constant voltage at AB, U1 (Rx voltage) and U2 (total voltage) are measured by different closing of switches S1 and S2.Based on the known R0, the Rx method is derived using Ohm's law and the voltage relationship between series circuits.
short_query: measuring unknown resistance with switches and a known resistor

id: P19. 17 电阻的测量（第二课时）_26
caption: This fragment summarizes various methods of resistance measurement, and the core application is Ohm's law and the serial-parallel circuit law.The emphasis is on the alternative method and the equivalent alternative method, and emphasizes the use of known resistors, voltmeters or ammeters to replace unknown resistors for measurement.
short_query: how to measure resistance using alternative and equivalent substitution methods

id: P19. 19  杠杆杠杆_视频课程_19
caption: This video analyzes the leverage balance conditions through experimental data.The product relationship between power and power arm, resistance and resistance arm is explained in detail, and the core physical law of power × power arm = resistance × resistance arm when lever balance is verified.
short_query: lever balance law power arm times resistance arm experiment

id: P19. 19  杠杆杠杆_视频课程_43
caption: This video explains the principle and classification of leverage, and distinguishes different types by bottle opener (work-saving leverage) and chopsticks (work-saving leverage) as examples.Summarize the definition of leverage and five elements: fulcrum, power, resistance, power arm, and resistance arm.
short_query: how leverage works with fulcrum power and resistance arm examples

id: P19. 19  杠杆杠杆_视频课程_36
caption: The video explains the classification of leverage and balance conditions.Taking the screwdriver prying nail as an example, it analyzes that its power arm is greater than the resistance arm. According to the lever balance conditions, it is proved that it is a labor-saving lever (power is less than resistance).
short_query: screwdriver prying a nail as a labor saving lever

id: P19. 19  杠杆杠杆_视频课程_37
caption: This video takes the bottle driver as an example to illustrate the labor-saving lever: the power arm is greater than the resistance arm, and labor-saving is achieved according to the lever balance conditions (F1<F2).It is also pointed out that labor-saving leverage and labor-saving distance are not saved, and the relationship between the lifting height of the heavy object and the moving distance of the power point will be explained.
short_query: how bottle openers work as labor saving levers in physics

id: P19. 19  杠杆杠杆_视频课程_28
caption: This video demonstrates a lever balance experiment, using a spring dynamometer to balance the lever horizontally.Record resistance F2=5N, resistance arm L2=0.15m, and power F1=3N, power arm L1=0.25m.The focus of teaching is to understand the leverage balance conditions and analyze it based on experimental data.
short_query: how to balance a lever using a spring dynamometer

id: P19. 19  杠杆杠杆_视频课程_6
caption: The video defines the power arm of the lever and the resistance arm, and focuses on teaching how to draw the force arm.The method includes: determining the action line of the fulcrum O and the force, and then making a perpendicular line from the fulcrum to the action line of the force, and the distance from the fulcrum to the vernal foot is the force arm.
short_query: how to draw the force arm of a lever

id: P19. 19  杠杆杠杆_视频课程_18
caption: This video analyzes experimental data and shows that the product of the power and the power arm during lever balance is a fixed value.It is concluded that when the resistance and resistance arm are constant, the power is inversely proportional to the power arm.Emphasize the leverage balance condition and verify the physical law through the image method.
short_query: lever balance condition power inversely proportional to power arm

id: P19. 19  杠杆杠杆_视频课程_34
caption: This video explains the principles and applications of leverage.The power arm with labor-saving lever is greater than the resistance arm, which is characterized by labor-saving but distance-consuming.The power arm of the labor lever is smaller than the resistance arm, which is characterized by labor but distance saving.By analyzing the size of the power arm and the resistance arm, different types of levers and their applications in life are distinguished.
short_query: different types of levers and their applications in life

id: P19. 19  杠杆杠杆_视频课程_24
caption: This video demonstrates the leverage balance experiment.By hanging the hook codes on both sides of the fulcrum as power (F1) and resistance (F2), the position is adjusted so that the lever reaches balance in the horizontal position.The data of the power arm (L1) and the resistance arm (L2) were recorded, aiming to experimentally explore and verify the lever equilibrium condition F1L1=F2L2.
short_query: experiment to verify the lever balance condition f1l1 f2l2

id: P19. 19 课题2 水的组成_6
caption: This video demonstrates hydrogen combustion experiments and emphasizes combustible gas purity testing.Pure hydrogen burns quietly in the air to produce water.It is pointed out that based on this experiment alone, the composition of water cannot be directly inferred, because air is a mixture, and further clarification of the reactants is needed.
short_query: testing hydrogen gas purity before burning to make water

id: P19. 19 课题2 水的组成_12
caption: Through quantitative water combination and decomposition experiments, Lavoisier proved that water is composed of hydrogen and oxygen elements. Water electrolysis only produces hydrogen and oxygen, reflecting the conservation of mass in chemical reactions.
short_query: lavoisier experiment proving water is made of hydrogen oxygen

id: P19. 19 课题2 水的组成_8
caption: This video demonstrates the electrolytic experiment, focusing on the composition of the Hoffman water electrolytic device (DC power supply, electrode) and preparation for water addition before the experiment, explaining the composition of water.
short_query: how to use a hoffman device for water electrolysis experiment

id: P19. 19 课题2 水的组成_3
caption: This video explores the composition of water, elucidating that physical changes alone cannot reveal their composition, and chemical changes are required.By reviewing the chemical reaction of sulfur and oxygen to generate sulfur dioxide, we demonstrate how to analyze the elemental composition of substances before and after chemical changes, laying the foundation for the subsequent exploration of the composition of water.
short_query: how chemical changes reveal the elemental composition of water

caption: This video clip defines the normal, incident angle and refractive angle in the refractive phenomenon of light.Through the experimental device of laser pen and dial, the relationship between the incident angle and the refractive angle is explored and compared with the reflection law.
short_query: relationship between incident angle and refractive angle experiment

caption: This video takes "watching the sky in a well" as a physical model to compare the influence of light propagation along a straight line and the refraction phenomenon of light on the field of view.The focus is on analyzing how the field of view of the frog at the bottom of the well will change after the well is filled with water, revealing the application of the principle of light refraction in explaining practical problems, and emphasizing the importance of physical modeling methods.
short_query: how refraction changes the field of view in a well

id: P2. 1.1 长度和时间的测量_10
caption: This video explains length measurement: it emphasizes that the scale must be parallel to the measured length, and the line of sight should be directly opposite the scale.When reading, the numerical estimate must be read to the next bit of the index value to ensure the accuracy of the measurement results.
short_query: how to measure length accurately with a ruler scale

id: P2. 1.1 长度和时间的测量_7
caption: This video explains length measurement, introduces common tools such as scale rulers, tape measurers, soft rulers, vernier calipers, and emphasizes selection based on measurement needs.Focus on explaining the standardized usage methods of scales, and guide students to observe their zero scale lines and measurement range before use to ensure measurement accuracy.
short_query: using rulers and tape measures for accurate length measurement

id: P2. 1.1 长度和时间的测量_15
caption: This video introduces the principle of high-precision timing of atomic clocks.Focus on teaching the use of stopwatches and readings: Detailed description of the stopwatch structure (start/stop, zero button, minute/second disc) and operations (start, stop, zero).Guide students to learn by analogy scales.
short_query: how to read a stopwatch and understand timing scales

id: P2. 1.1 长度和时间的测量_19
caption: Video distinguishes errors from errors in measurements, pointing out that errors are inevitable but can be reduced by selecting precision instruments, multiple measurements to average and improved measurement methods.A case of measuring the diameter of a coin through a scale to demonstrate strategies for reducing errors.
short_query: how to reduce errors in science measurements using better tools

caption: This video focuses on explaining the concept of internal energy.By comparing mechanical energy, it is pointed out that even if the mechanical energy is zero, it must have internal energy due to the unchanging irregular thermal movement of all molecules inside it.It emphasizes that any object has an internal energy that is non-zero due to the thermal motion of molecules.
short_query: understanding internal energy and thermal motion in objects

caption: This video defines internal energy as the sum of the thermal kinetic energy and molecular potential energy of all molecules of an object, emphasizing that all objects have internal energy.Explain that temperature is the physical quantity of hot and cold, and through the contact between hot and cold copper blocks, explain the principle of heat transfer: heat transfer occurs when objects in contact with different temperatures.
short_query: what is internal energy and how does heat transfer work

caption: This video takes "Heating Water Bag" as an example to explain the concept of internal energy.Analyze the phenomenon of the increase in the temperature of both hands, the increase in the internal energy, the decrease in the temperature of the hot water bag, and the decrease in the internal energy, and introduce heat transfer to cause the transfer of internal energy.
short_query: how heat transfer works with a hot water bag example

caption: The video explains heat transfer: energy is transmitted from high-temperature objects to low-temperature objects.The energy in high-temperature objects decreases, the energy in low-temperature objects increases, and the temperature in low-temperature objects increases.The condition for heat transfer is that there is a temperature difference.
short_query: how heat energy moves from hot to cold objects

caption: This video focuses on internal energy, explains heat transfer through life examples, and focuses on distinguishing between three physical quantities that are easily confused by temperature, internal energy and heat.
short_query: difference between temperature internal energy and heat examples

id: P2. 2  凸透镜成像规律凸透镜成像规律_视频课程_32
caption: This video illustrates the imaging rules of convex lenses.Through the analyte distance, it is pointed out that the object distance is greater than one times the focal length to form a real image, and the object distance is less than one times the focal length to form a virtual image; when the object distance is greater than two times the focal length, it becomes a reduced real image.
short_query: how convex lenses form real and virtual images explained

id: P2. 2  凸透镜成像规律凸透镜成像规律_视频课程_39
caption: The video explains the imaging rules of convex lenses.Light parallel to the main optical axis converges at the focus through a convex lens.The convex lens can deflect all passing light in the direction of the main optical axis, and has a convergence effect.
short_query: how convex lenses focus parallel light rays and converge

id: P2. 2  凸透镜成像规律凸透镜成像规律_视频课程_35
caption: This video explains the common reasons why the light screen cannot receive clear images in the imaging law experiment of convex lenses: if the object distance is within one focal length, a virtual image cannot be received; if the object distance is equal to one focal length, the image will not be formed.
short_query: why no clear image on screen in convex lens experiment

id: P2. 2  凸透镜成像规律凸透镜成像规律_视频课程_27
caption: This video summarizes the imaging rules of convex lenses: the object is between one focal length and two focal length, and the light screen forms an inverted enlarged real image; the object is at one focal length, and the light screen cannot image.
short_query: convex lens imaging rules between one and two focal lengths

id: P2. 2  凸透镜成像规律凸透镜成像规律_视频课程_34
caption: This video paragraph explains in detail the law of convex lenses to form a real image, pointing out that when the object distance decreases (the object is close), the image distance increases (the image becomes farther), and the image becomes larger.It aims to help students to deeply understand the changing relationship between object distance, image distance and image size in convex lens imaging.
short_query: how object distance affects image size in convex lenses

id: P2. 2  凸透镜成像规律凸透镜成像规律_视频课程_31
caption: The video explains the imaging rules of convex lenses: when the object is located at the two focal length (2f), it forms a large real image such as an inverted stand-up; between f and 2f, it forms an inverted magnification real image; at the focal point f, it does not form an image; within f, it forms an upright magnification virtual image, and the virtual image is the same as the object.
short_query: how convex lenses form images at different focal lengths

id: P2. 2  凸透镜成像规律凸透镜成像规律_视频课程_26
caption: This video explains the imaging rules of convex lenses and is demonstrated through experimental demonstration.The focus is on analyzing the object at twice the focal length, and the focal length on the other side of the lens is to obtain a large real image of the inverted and inverted image.Also mention the situation where objects are placed between one focal length and two focal lengths, and find clear images by moving the light screen.
short_query: convex lens imaging experiment with object at twice focal length

id: P2. 2  凸透镜成像规律凸透镜成像规律_视频课程_20
caption: This video discusses the imaging rules of **convex lenses**.Taking the magnifying glass as an example, it explains its function of magnifying text to aid reading, leading to in-depth learning of the imaging principle of convex lenses.
short_query: how convex lenses work and imaging rules of magnifying glasses

id: P2. 2  凸透镜成像规律凸透镜成像规律_视频课程_5
caption: Video analysis of the convergence effect of convex lenses (light deviates toward the main optical axis) and the divergence effect of concave lenses (light deviates from the main optical axis), clarifying the optical principles of lenses.
short_query: how convex and concave lenses bend light rays

id: P2. 2  第一节 物态变化 温度_3
caption: This video explains the three basic physical states of a substance: solid state, liquid state and gaseous state, and explains its shape and volume characteristics: solid state has a fixed shape and volume; liquid state has a fixed volume but no fixed shape; gaseous state has no fixed shape and volume.The change in the solid to liquid state is demonstrated through the melting of ice.
short_query: difference between solid liquid and gas shape and volume

id: P2. 2  第一节 物态变化 温度_10
caption: This video introduces the structural differences between the thermometer and the laboratory thermometer.Focus on explaining the definition of Celsius: formulated by Swedish physicist Anders Sercius, stipulating that under standard atmospheric pressure, the temperature of the ice-water mixture is 0℃ and the boiling water temperature is 100℃.
short_query: difference between clinical and laboratory thermometers and celsius scale

id: P2. 2  第一节 物态变化 温度_5
caption: The video explains that physics introduces the concept of "temperature", which defines it as a physical quantity that represents the degree of hot and cold of an object, and the commonly used symbol is t or T.In addition, the ancients' understanding of hot and cold is briefly mentioned.
short_query: what is temperature in physics and the symbols used

id: P2. 2  第一节 物态变化 温度_9
caption: This video explains the working principle of the thermometer: using liquid to expand and contract.The liquid expands when it encounters heat and shrinks when it encounters cold.The structure of laboratory thermometers (glass bubbles, capillaries, scales) and commonly used temperature measurement liquids, such as alcohol, kerosene, mercury, etc. are introduced.
short_query: how thermometers work using liquid expansion and contraction

caption: This video demonstrates the short circuit phenomenon in the circuit through experiments.When the positive and negative electrodes of the power supply are directly connected, the current flows directly from the positive electrode to the negative electrode without passing through the electrical appliance, causing the electrical appliance to stop working.A clear short circuit refers to the circuit state where current flows directly from the positive electrode of the power supply to the negative electrode without passing through the electrical appliance.
short_query: short circuit experiment connecting positive and negative electrodes

caption: The video introduces the concept of circuit components through the case of mini electric fans and explains its function with the motor as an example.Emphasize the importance of learning to understand the various components in circuits and their correct connection.
short_query: how motors and circuit components work in electric fans

caption: This video summarizes the composition, component function, connection method, three states (path, circuit breaker, short circuit) and short circuit hazards of the circuit, and learns to draw the circuit diagram.
short_query: how to draw circuit diagrams and understand circuit states

caption: The video points out the complex circuit analysis problems and introduces the concept of circuit diagrams.The connection relationship can be clearly displayed through standardized symbols, and circuit analysis and troubleshooting can be simplified.
short_query: using standardized symbols to understand complex circuit diagrams

caption: The video explains in detail two basic states of a simple circuit: a path (or closed circuit) when the switch is closed and the current is turned on; a circuit is off when the switch is turned off and the current is not turned on.Through small fan demonstration, help students understand and distinguish between paths and circuit breakers.
short_query: how closed and open circuits work with a small fan

caption: This video explains three states of the circuit: path (closed), open (open), and short circuit, and focuses on defining the short circuit as a direct current through the power supply without using electrical appliances.Explain a simple circuit example composed of power supply, switches and electrical appliances.
short_query: difference between open closed and short circuits explained

id: P2. 2  课题2 金属的化学性质（第1课时）_3
caption: The video compares the reaction of magnesium and oxygen, demonstrating that the red heat melts but does not drip when the aluminum foil is heated.This phenomenon reveals the protective effect of dense oxide film on aluminum surface and reflects the special chemical properties of aluminum.
short_query: why aluminum foil melts but does not drip when heated

id: P2. 2  课题2 金属的化学性质（第1课时）_10
caption: The video explores the reactions of Mg, Zn, Fe, Cu with dilute hydrochloric acid and dilute sulfuric acid. Through the comparison of phenomena, the order of metal activity is revealed: Mg>Zn>Fe>Cu.
short_query: comparing metal reactivity series with magnesium zinc iron copper

id: P2. 2  课题2 金属的化学性质（第1课时）_6
caption: This video analyzes that aluminum products are corrosion-resistant due to the formation of dense alumina film on the surface.It is pointed out that mechanical damage can easily destroy the protective film, causing the inner metal to be oxidized and corroded.The content involves the reaction between various metals such as magnesium, aluminum, iron, and copper and oxygen.
short_query: how dense alumina film protects aluminum products from corrosion

id: P2. 2 课题1 物质的变化和性质_2
caption: This video introduces the concept of material change through the three-state changes of water (physical changes), iron rust and wood combustion (chemical changes), guides students to distinguish between physical and chemical changes, and previews the experiment to explore the principles of chemistry.
short_query: how to distinguish between physical and chemical changes in science

id: P2. 2 课题1 物质的变化和性质_5
caption: This video demonstrates physical changes (no new substance generation) through paraffin melting, and mentions that blue floc precipitation was observed in experiments to distinguish chemical changes of substances, emphasizing that the presence or absence of new substances is the key to judging changes.
short_query: difference between physical and chemical changes with paraffin melting example

id: P2. 2 课题1 物质的变化和性质_8
caption: Video distinguishes physical changes from chemical changes.Physical changes (such as tri-state changes) do not generate new substances; chemical changes generate new substances, which are often accompanied by colors, gas, precipitation, heat, luminescence and other phenomena.These are the basis for judging chemical changes.Teaching focus: concept and phenomenon recognition.
short_query: difference between physical and chemical changes with examples

id: P2. 2 课题1 物质的变化和性质_21
caption: Through experimental exploration, this video explains the core concepts of material changes and properties.The content focuses on the composition, structure and changing laws of matter, aiming to lay the foundation for chemistry learning and guide students to apply knowledge.
short_query: introduction to material changes and properties for students

caption: This video is taught by Teacher Tian Yajuan, and mainly learns the core concept of "elasticity" in junior high school physics.The beginning is through a magic performance (ribbon turns into straight cylinder), aiming to stimulate students' interest in elastic learning.
short_query: junior high physics elasticity lecture by teacher tian yajuan

caption: This video focuses on elastic properties and focuses on explaining the principles, structure and usage techniques of spring force gauge.The content emphasizes the attention to the index value when using it. If the index value is 0.2N, the number of display is 2.4N.It aims to help students master the concept of elasticity and the correct use of spring dynamometers.
short_query: how to use a spring force gauge and read values

caption: Elastic force is the force generated when an object undergoes elastic deformation and returns to its original state.Its direction is consistent with the deformation recovery direction, and its size is proportional to the degree of deformation within the elastic limit (such as a spring).Exceeding the elastic limit, the object cannot be restored to its original state.
short_query: how objects return to original state after elastic deformation

caption: The video explains that elasticity is generated by elastic deformation through tests on desktop heavy objects and extruded glass bottles.Support force is an elastic force. Changes in water column can indicate slight deformation and volume change. It is an experimental method for judging elastic deformation.
short_query: how to see elastic deformation in glass bottle experiments

id: P20. 11  课题2 化学与可持续发展（第1课时）_1
caption: This video explores chemistry and sustainable development, emphasizing the rational use of natural resources under ecological protection.The teaching focus is to analyze the use of natural resources such as air, water, fossil fuels, metal minerals from the perspective of material composition and transformation.
short_query: how chemistry helps us use natural resources sustainably

id: P20. 11  课题2 化学与可持续发展（第1课时）_18
caption: This video discusses chemistry and sustainable development, and focuses on the electrolytic seawater hydrogen production technology.This technology uses green electricity to directly prepare hydrogen from seawater, breaking through the traditional hydrogen production limits of freshwater.As a clean energy preparation method, it is of great significance to ensuring energy supply and achieving the dual-carbon goal, laying the foundation for industrial application.
short_query: how to make hydrogen from seawater using green electricity

id: P20. 11  课题2 化学与可持续发展（第1课时）_23
caption: This video is the first lesson on the theme of Chemistry and Sustainability.It mainly expounds the important role of chemistry in the comprehensive utilization of resources and its significant significance in the sustainable development of energy, emphasizing the contribution of chemistry to society and the environment.

id: P42. 40  在科学探究中怎样提出可探究的科学问题_8
caption: This video explains that in scientific inquiry, in response to the situation where multiple factors affect the dependent variable, the **control variable method should be used to disassemble complex problems into multiple **single factor problems to raise **explorable scientific questions**.For example, when exploring liquid pressure, problems related to single factors such as liquid density and object depth can be raised.

id: P42. 40  在科学探究中怎样提出可探究的科学问题_3
caption: The video emphasizes the importance of raising “explorable scientific questions” in scientific inquiry.From the contributions of scientists such as Aristotle to Hawking in the field of physics, we can see that raising questions is a key criterion for measuring scientific research capabilities.The vitality of a discipline lies in constantly raising new questions, and good scientific questions are the cornerstone of national scientific and technological progress and come from the development of the times.

caption: This video explains the naming rules of solutions, pointing out that when water is used as a solvent, the solution is named after a solute (such as sodium carbonate solution and potassium permanganate solution); when the solvent is non-water, the name of the solvent must be specified (such as alcohol solution of iodine and carbon disulfide solution of white phosphorus), and the solution naming knowledge must be consolidated through exercises of identifying solutes and solvents.

id: P6. 4  课题1 溶液及其应用（第1课时）_2
caption: This video introduces the concept of solution, taking seawater as an example.Through experiments, the dissolution process and phenomena of sucrose and sodium chloride in water were observed, and the dissolution method and essence of substances were explored, laying the foundation for subsequent systematic learning of solutions.

id: P6. 6  弹力弹力_视频课程_5
caption: This video introduces the deformation caused to objects by force from life examples such as egg-kneading and car impact.According to whether the deformation can be completely restored after force is removed, deformation is divided into elastic deformation and plastic deformation, and the basis for distinguishing the two is clarified.

id: P6. 6  弹力弹力_视频课程_19
caption: The video explains elastic force, which refers to the force generated when an object deforms and tries to restore its original state.Taking the hanging line pulling ball as an example, explain the direction of elastic force and the relationship between the action force and the reaction force.It is emphasized that the generation of elastic force must be accompanied by deformation, and the deformation may be small.

id: P6. 6  弹力弹力_视频课程_23

id: P6. 6  弹力弹力_视频课程_10
caption: This video explains the physical "elastic limit": the maximum range that an object can completely restore its original state after deformation, and it cannot be restored beyond this limit.And define "elastic force": the force generated on the contact object when an object that is in contact with each other and undergoes elastic deformation.

id: P6. 6  弹力弹力_视频课程_22
caption: This video analyzes the deformation mechanism of the spring when it is subjected to tension, and explains that the total elongation is the result of the accumulation of slight deformations in each circle.The focus of the course is to explore the relationship between the tension force applied to the spring and its elongation through experiments, aiming to explain in-depth the physical laws of elasticity and deformation, laying the foundation for understanding Hook's law.

id: P6. 6  弹力弹力_视频课程_20

id: P6. 6  弹力弹力_视频课程_11
caption: This video explains the concept of elasticity, the conditions (deformation) and its universality.Through the example of a hand-pressing spring, the direction of elastic force (opposite to the direction of deformation recovery) and the point of action are analyzed, and a schematic diagram of force is demonstrated, emphasizing support force as a way to represent elastic force.

caption: This video explores the relationship between resistance and conductor cross-sectional area and sets the cross-sectional area as an independent variable.The material, length and power supply voltage of the conductor were controlled experimentally, and the current was measured using a nickel-chromium wire access circuit to analyze the resistance changes.

caption: By comparing the nickel-chromium wire and Concopper wire access circuits, the experiment demonstrates the influence of different conductor materials on circuit current and bulb brightness, revealing the correlation between conductor resistance and material.

caption: The video explains the resistance units of ohms, kiloohms and megohms (1kΩ=10³Ω, 1MΩ=10³kΩ), explains the fixed-value resistance and circuit symbols, and lists the typical resistance values ​​of small bulbs and incandescent filaments.

id: P60. 第六章 质量和密度复习（第一课时）_6
caption: This video reviews the usage specifications for balance measurement quality: it emphasizes that the object to be measured cannot exceed the balance range; tweezers must be used to add or subtract weights, and direct contact with hands is strictly prohibited; wet objects, chemicals and liquids cannot be placed directly on the plate, and auxiliary containers such as beakers and white paper are required.

id: P61. 第六章 质量和密度复习（第二课时）_3
caption: Video review balance measuring cylinder method to measure liquid density: measure the mass of the empty beaker m1, the total mass of the beaker added m2, and the liquid volume V.Calculate m=m2-m1, and the formula ρ=m/V.The measurement deviation and reasons were analyzed, and it was pointed out that the residual liquid in the beaker would cause the volume measurement to be small.

id: P61. 第六章 质量和密度复习（第二课时）_15
caption: This video reviews the concept of quality and density.Taking copper wire as an example, we explain the measurement of mass (178 grams) by weight, and use the density formula V=m/ρ to calculate the volume (20 cubic centimeters), and then calculate the length of copper wire (4000 cm).Emphasize that density is an inherent property of matter.

id: P61. 第六章 质量和密度复习（第二课时）_5
caption: The video explains the method of eliminating errors in liquid density measurement.By adjusting the experimental sequence, that is, first use a measuring cylinder to measure the volume V of the liquid, and then use a balance to measure the mass of the cup and the remaining liquid, the error caused by the residual container wall is effectively solved.This operation counts the mass of residual liquid into the residual mass, improving the accuracy of density measurements.

id: P61. 第六章 质量和密度复习（第二课时）_17

caption: The essence of frictional electricity is the transfer of electrons between different objects, not charge creation.If a glass rod rubs against silk, glass will be positively charged because it loses electrons, and silk will be negatively charged because it gets electrons.The total charge is conserved, and the difference in electron binding ability determines the direction of transfer.
